items.reason: All 20 decision areas listed in `docs/decisions/decisions-needed.md` (DEC-001 through DEC-020) have corresponding decisions in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` with all six required fields. DEC-016 through DEC-020 cover the platform-shape decisions (dictionary direction, canonical fields, enums, privacy classes, migrations) with populated `projects_to` lists naming concrete artifact paths.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001-runtime-slice-schedule (target 2026-05-15) and PEND-005-deno-check-in-ci (target 2026-05-03), each with owner (Andy), target_date, blocker, and one-line rationale. Both target dates are in the future relative to the 2026-04-26 research date.

Each decision's consequences section names concrete artifacts removed: DEC-016 'Removes five-way hand-edited dictionary reconciliation', DEC-017 'Removes runtime/docs conversion between email, LTI email claims, actor identifiers, and candidate labels', DEC-018 'Removes per-API parsers for OneRoster names, LTI URIs, Caliper roles, and QTI values', DEC-019 'Removes generator branches that interpret open-ended privacy placeholders', DEC-020 'Removes duplicated FK truth across SQL, dictionary prose, docs'. DEC-001 through DEC-015 follow the same 'Removes the need for...' pattern with named artifacts.

Each decision brackets the design space with credible alternatives. DEC-016 considers (a) on-demand generation without committed per-spec files (more aggressive), (b) per-spec files canonical with synthesized shared docs (more permissive), (c) shared dictionary canonical with generated committed projections (chosen). DEC-001 contrasts collapsing all identities into one `person` (aggressive) vs. separate tables per standard (permissive) vs. canonical person + agent (chosen). These are real architectural positions production platforms take, not strawmen.

DEC-016 declares `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` the upstream canonical model and per-spec dictionaries as projections, but no `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` exists. The committed generators (`scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py`, `generate_qti_core.py`, `generate_case_core.py`, etc.) read FROM the per-spec `dictionary/*.v1.json` files, not from the shared seed. The direction is still inverted: per-spec files are hand-edited and authoritative, contradicting DEC-016. Traced cause: DEC-016 decided but not projected into code; needs `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` and an expanded shared seed.

Per-spec fields do not carry `canonical_field_id`. OneRoster `person.email`, Caliper `caliper_actor.identifier_value`, and integration `lti_membership.email` each have `sourceStandard` and `decision_id` but no `canonical_field_id` pointing at a single shared email field in `data/data-dictionary.seed.json`. DEC-017's `projects_to` lists this exact reconciliation but the data has not been written. Traced cause: DEC-017 decided but not projected; the shared dictionary lacks a canonical `person.email` field and per-spec fields lack `canonical_field_id` references.

Identity-shaped objects across specs (OneRoster `person`, Caliper `caliper_actor`, LTI `lti_membership`, plus the QTI Candidate concept) carry no `canonical_object_id` reference to a single shared identity entity. The shared dictionary (`data/data-dictionary.seed.json`) does not contain a canonical identity entity with its own object record, fields, allowed values, and projections — DEC-001's resolution is prose-only. Traced cause: DEC-001 needs a data projection; the shared dictionary must contain a canonical identity object that every spec-side identity points to via `canonical_object_id`.

Per-spec enum fields use inline `allowed_values` arrays and do not reference a `shared_enum_id`. OneRoster `person.primary_role` declares its values inline; LTI `lti_launch.roles` and Caliper actor types declare independent enums. There is no shared enum table mapping spec-native values bidirectionally to canonical values. DEC-018 was decided but the dictionaries have not been refactored. Traced cause: DEC-018 decided but not projected; need a shared enum table in `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` and `shared_enum_id` references on every per-spec enum field.

DEC-019 explicitly states `depends_on_contents` and `depends_on_entity` are invalid placeholders, yet `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json` still uses them: `caliper_entity.name` has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_entity"`, `caliper_entity.description` has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_entity"`, and `caliper_entity.extensions` has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_contents"`. The data directly contradicts the decision. Traced cause: Caliper dictionary fields must be resolved into concrete privacy classes from the closed list (`public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, `system`).

Dictionary object records do not carry an explicit `relationships` array declaring FK targets, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. FK information lives only in field descriptions (e.g. OneRoster `course.org_id` describes that it references organizations) and in hand-written `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql`. No generator produces migrations from the dictionary; the migration is the source of truth for relational structure. Traced cause: DEC-020 decided but not projected; need `relationships` on every dictionary object plus a migration generator with CI diff check.

Every field in the per-spec dictionaries carries `decision_id` (e.g. OneRoster `person.email` cites DEC-001-person-agent-subject, `course.id` cites DEC-007-identifier-crosswalk). However, allowed-value entries (enums) do not carry `decision_id` — OneRoster's role values and status values have only `sourceStandard`. Object-level records also do not carry `decision_id` (only their fields do). Privacy classes appear as inline strings on fields without per-class decision linkage. Traced cause: extend `check_dictionary_artifacts.py` to require `decision_id` on objects, allowed_values, and privacy_classes, and add the citations.

Object/field/enum reference pages are generated by `generate_*_core.py` from per-spec dictionaries (verified by `check_dictionary_artifacts.py`). However, hand-written architecture pages (`docs/lead-spec-accounting.md`, `docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md`, `docs/spec-gap-backlog.md`) make load-bearing claims about fields, enum values, and runtime behavior in prose without linking to dictionary anchors per claim. The site portal links to generated pages but the descriptive prose isn't anchor-linked. Traced cause: hand-edited descriptive prose must link to dictionary anchors for every load-bearing claim, and a doc-generation pass over the prose architecture pages should be added.

The portal renders structured decision cards from the canonical register (`site/index.html` `#decisions` section parses `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` and shows id, choice, consequences, and projects_to per decision). The decision register itself renders as HTML at `site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html` with anchors per decision, and individual concept docs (e.g. lead-spec-accounting.md) link to specific DEC-IDs explaining the rationale and what each decision simplifies.

`docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` does not exist. The README and `site/docs/index.html` index list no buildability guide. The portal's `#guide` section contains a high-level 'Platform Provides / App Developer Owns / First Integration Path' card but no copy-pasteable curl/SQL walking through (a) creating a school, (b) enrolling a student, (c) posting a grade, (d) linking to a CASE standard, (e) reading a Caliper activity feed. Traced cause: write `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` with executable curl/SQL for all five steps and link every field/enum to its dictionary entry.

The portal builds a docs index at `site/docs/index.html` linking to rendered pages, and `site/index.html` provides primary navigation. No link checker is referenced in `VERIFY.md` and no automated proof of zero 404s or full reachability is committed. Generated dictionary pages exist as duplicates (the gap backlog item 25 explicitly notes 'Audit generated dictionary portal pages for duplicate or orphaned HTML' is open). Traced cause: add a link-checker step to VERIFY.md and resolve the duplicate generated dictionary HTML noted in spec-gap-backlog.md item 25.

A layperson reading only `docs/` cannot produce executable artifacts for the five required steps. Gap 1: no `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` walks through school creation, enrollment, grading, CASE alignment, or Caliper feed reading. Gap 2: posting a grade requires the gradebook-bulk-submit Edge Function but the docs don't show a layperson developer how to obtain a tenant-scoped JWT. Gap 3: linking to a CASE standard URI is undocumented because CASE runtime is deferred (per DEC-012) — no endpoint exists for this. Gap 4: reading a Caliper activity feed has no documented read path; only an authenticated receipt path exists. Traced causes: missing `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` (docs_include_buildability_guide), missing canonical email/identity unification (dictionary_resolves_cross_spec_overlaps, dictionary_unifies_identity), CASE/Caliper runtime deferred per DEC-012 with no doc-only fallback path.

`scripts/evaluate_platform.py`'s prompt instructs the LLM to return only `id, status, reason, evidence_paths, blocked_prerequisites` — there is no `traced_cause` field. The output verdict structure assembled in `call_evaluator()` does not include `traced_cause`, and there is no `buildability_gaps` array. The override file at `docs/loop-overrides.md` explicitly mandates this update but it has not been implemented. Traced cause: update the evaluator prompt to require a `traced_cause` field on every non-pass item and to emit a `buildability_gaps` array, then update the verdict assembly code accordingly.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json` so the evaluator runs each iteration. The report contains `done`, per-item `status`, `counts`, and `runtimePrereqStatus`. However, it lacks `traced_cause` on non-pass items and lacks a `buildability_gaps` array — both required by the rubric. Traced cause: extend `evaluate_platform.py` to emit these fields (same fix as evaluator_traces_failures_backward).

`scripts/codex_loop.py` defines `read_overrides()` reading `docs/loop-overrides.md`, `overrides_paused()` checking for a literal `PAUSE` token on its own line outside HTML comments, and `format_overrides_for_prompt()` which injects the cleaned content into the iteration prompt under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE — these supersede rubric defaults...)' section. The PAUSE check runs at the top of each iteration before Codex is invoked, halting the loop before the next run.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` checks `if after_eval and after_eval.get('done')` after each iteration and prints 'Evaluator reports `done` after this iteration. Stopping the loop.' before returning. There is also a pre-iteration check `if before_eval and before_eval.get('done')`. Termination references the LLM evaluator's `done` flag, not a numeric score threshold; the advisory score gate is explicitly described as advisory. Hard caps (`--max-iterations`, `--stall-limit`) act as safety rails.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.
